conjecture_title: Open Problem: Polynomial-time Coloring on even-hole-free graphs
paper_title: Tree Independence Number IV. Even-hole-free Graphs
summary: The question of whether a polynomial-time algorithm for computing the chromatic number exists on even-hole-free graphs remains open as of May 2026. The source paper (arXiv:2407.08927) established only quasi-polynomial-time results via tree independence number, explicitly leaving the polynomial-time coloring question unresolved. No subsequent paper was found that resolves this problem; related polynomial-time results exist only for strict subclasses such as (cap, even-hole)-free graphs and even-hole-free graphs with no star cutset.
search_queries: even-hole-free graphs coloring chromatic number polynomial-time algorithm 2024 2025
arxiv 2407.08927 citing papers tree independence number even-hole-free
polynomial-time coloring even-hole-free graphs chromatic number algorithm 2025 arxiv
notes: No follow-up paper resolving the conjecture was found across three web searches and two WebFetch calls to the arxiv abstract page and Semantic Scholar. Polynomial-time coloring is known for restricted subclasses (e.g., (cap, even-hole)-free graphs, even-hole-free graphs with no star cutset) but the full even-hole-free class remains open. The paper's quasi-polynomial-time QPTAS via tree independence number (tree-independence number O(log^10 n)) does not resolve chromatic number computation.
